title: Scientific Measurement
author: Viacheslav Chebotarev
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0

steps.instruction: Since the spring of this year, I began to study quantum mechanics, read literature on physics, and study programming more deeply. The discussion about describing scientific measurements and comparing the different quantitative characteristics that define them is very interesting. This is probably a matter of opinion, so it's probably important to just state your opinion. Physics is an experimental science. Experiment is a source of knowledge and a criterion for the truth of any hypotheses and theories. Currently, physical methods of research and control are widely used in all branches of technology without exception. Therefore, it is very important, during the learning process, to master a wide range of physical techniques, gain experience in using various measuring instruments and, no less important, learn to correctly assess the errors that inevitably arise. The knowledge, skills and abilities that should allow solving these problems are intended to be given to the student by working in the laboratory. Performing laboratory work is in many ways similar to conducting a scientific experiment, has the same goals and consists of similar steps.
